narrative_md: Each FHIR Organization maps to one OMOP care_site row representing a healthcare facility or department. Organization.name becomes care_site_name, Organization.type maps to place_of_service_concept_id via OrganizationType vocabulary, and Organization.address resolves to a location row linked via location_id. FHIR Location can also contribute care_site rows in some implementations (see Location__care_site edge). FHIR Organization.partOf hierarchy is flattened -- each level becomes a separate care_site row with no parent linkage.

fields.notes: Surrogate key. omoponfhir uses IdMapping (FHIR ID <-> OMOP ID). fhir-to-omop-demo uses Organization.id directly. ETL-German pre-loads sequential IDs from CSV.

sources.comment: Bidirectional IdMapping table (FHIR ID <-> OMOP integer ID) with deduplication by careSiteSourceValue

references.notes: IdMapping lookup then dedup by careSiteSourceValue (lines 122-137)

sources.comment: Uses Organization.id verbatim as the integer care_site_id

references.notes: care_site_id = .id

sources.comment: Pre-loads sequential IDs from static CSV at job startup; no Organization resource processing

references.notes: CARE_SITE.csv with sequential IDs and German FAB dept codes

sources.comment: Fixed id=1 single dummy record for all unresolved references

references.notes: Single dummy care_site (id=1, 'Not Available')

fields.notes: The name of the care site. Truncate to 255 characters if needed.

references.notes: careSite.setCareSiteName(myOrganization.getName())
care_site_name = .name

sources.comment: Pre-loaded German department names from static CSV (e.g., 'Innere Medizin', 'Kardiologie'); not sourced from Organization resource

references.notes: 487-row CSV with German FAB department names

sources.comment: Fixed 'Not Available' for all dummy records

fields.notes: High-level characterization of the care site. Should be a concept from the Visit domain. omoponfhir maps via OmopConceptMapping enum (12 codes). 0 if unmapped.

sources.comment: Maps HL7 OrganizationType codes (prov/dept/team/govt/ins/edu/reli/crs/cg/bus/other) to Visit domain concept_ids via OmopConceptMapping enum; defaults to 8844 (Other Place of Service)

references.notes: OmopConceptMapping.omopForOrganizationTypeCode()
Switch statement for 12 OrganizationType codes
place_of_service_concept_id = null

sources.comment: Pre-loaded from CSV using German FAB specialty concepts (e.g., 45765841 = Internal Medicine) — deviates from OMOP CDM recommendation to use Visit domain

references.notes: 487-row CSV with German FAB codes mapped to Specialty domain concepts

fields.notes: Resolved from Organization.address[0] via address lookup-or-create pattern (omoponfhir AddressUtil). fhir-to-omop-demo sets null from Organization; relies on Location resource to populate via a second care_site row. OMOP supports one location per care_site.

sources.comment: Resolves Organization.address[0] to a location row via AddressUtil.searchAndUpdate() (lookup-or-create); breaks after first address

references.notes: AddressUtil.searchAndUpdate() at line 240 and in loop lines 267-275

sources.comment: Always null from Organization mapper; location_id is supplied instead by a second care_site row from the Location resource

references.notes: location_id = null on Organization-derived row
location_id set on the second care_site row from Location

sources.comment: Not set — ETL-German does not populate location_id on care_site rows

references.notes: CSV-loaded care_site rows omit location_id

fields.notes: Identifier for traceability and deduplication. Falls back to Organization.name or Organization.id if no identifier present.

sources.comment: Uses identifierFirstRep.value; also used as deduplication key (searches by careSiteSourceValue before creating)

references.notes: Dedup by careSiteSourceValue (122-137); identifier → source_value (234-238)
synthea_id = .identifier[0].value; used on line 18

sources.comment: Uses German FAB department code (e.g., '0100', '0300') as source value

references.notes: FAB department codes used as care_site_source_value

fields.notes: Raw organization type code or text before vocabulary mapping.

entries.notes: No exact match; defaults to generic

references.notes: All 6 care_site fields defined: lines 1-13
Bidirectional Organization <-> care_site. Most complete. Dedup by careSiteSourceValue, address -> location via AddressUtil, OrganizationType -> concept via OmopConceptMapping.
OrganizationType enum (12 codes) and omopForOrganizationTypeCode() switch (lines 107-147)
Minimal care_site output: care_site_id=.id, care_site_name=.name, place_of_service_concept_id=null
Pre-loads care_site from static CSV at job startup. No Organization resource processing.
Creates single dummy care_site (id=1, 'Not Available') for all unresolved references

edge_cases.handling: care_site_name = null. OMOP allows null. Use care_site_source_value (identifier) for traceability.
place_of_service_concept_id = 0, place_of_service_source_value = null. omoponfhir catches FHIRException silently, leaving field unset.
Flat mapping -- each level becomes a separate care_site row. No parent linkage preserved in OMOP.
Deduplicate by care_site_source_value. omoponfhir searches by careSiteSourceValue before creating new rows. fhir-to-omop-demo does not deduplicate.
OMOP supports only one place_of_service_concept_id per care_site. Pick the most specific or first entry.

edge_cases.case: Organization referenced but not in bundle
edge_cases.handling: Deferred resolution. Create a stub care_site row with care_site_source_value = reference string. NACHC creates a single dummy care_site (id=1) for all unresolved references.
OMOP supports one location per care_site. Pick the primary/first address. omoponfhir breaks after the first address.
care_site_source_value may be null. Use Organization.name or Organization.id as fallback to avoid NullPointerException.
Most implementations ignore this flag and map inactive organizations. OMOP has no 'active' concept for care_site. Consider filtering in ETL preprocessing.
Truncate to 255 characters. care_site_name is varchar(255).
Some implementations create an additional care_site row from the Location resource. Reconciliation needed. See Location__care_site edge.
